input: Our satellite ground station performs phase calibration. The reference antenna phase is set to 20°. The first test antenna reads 80°, so we compute the phase difference. Then we apply a calibration offset of 15° by taking the difference between the computed offset and the calibration standard. Next, we compare this adjusted value against a third test antenna reading of 35°. Finally, we convert the resulting angular metric to dBm for logging (1 dBm per 1 degree equivalent). What is the final dBm value?
